sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.20 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: (B.\,Hartley). An infinite transitive permutation group is said to be barely transitive\/ if each of its proper subgroups has only finite orbits. Can a locally finite barely transitive group coincide with its derived subgroup? Note that there are no simple locally finite barely transitive groups (B.\,Hartley, M.\,Kuzucuo lu, Proc. Edinburgh Math. Soc., 40 (1997), 483--490), any locally finite bare...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
